sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection of the headline's use of 'Leftist' as a politically loaded label. Sonnet correctly identifies the issue.
Matches my detection of the author's ideologically loaded characterization of Australia's government in their own prose.

sonnet_review.biasedText: cracking down even more on gun ownership
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection of 'cracking down even more' as loaded, interpretive language. I categorized it as Spin, but the substance is the same.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Australian leaders have proposed a new measure that would limit the number of guns a person could own, according to the Associated Press.
sonnet_review.reason: This detection is a stretch. The sentence itself is a neutral factual statement attributed to AP. Claiming its placement 'minimizes' the policy is speculative and doesn't identify bias in the text itself. The article's structural issue is better captured by the overall framing emphasizing existing restrictions, not the placement of this particular sentence.

gpt_review.biasedText: Australia's Leftist Leaders Call For 'Tougher Gun Laws' After Antisemitic Massacre
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of 'Leftist' in the headline as a politically loaded descriptor. Different bias type label but same substantive detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: Albanese responded to the terrorist attack by cracking down even more on gun ownership
gpt_review.reason: Directly matches my detection of 'cracking down even more' as loaded, interpretive spin in the author's own voice.

gpt_review.biasedText: Australia already has some of the most restrictive gun laws in the world
gpt_review.reason: While I flagged this more as Slant (the structural framing of existing laws as already strict to undermine new proposals), GPT's point about 'most restrictive' being loaded has some merit. However, 'restrictive' is fairly factual/descriptive given Australia's actual laws, and replacing it with 'strict' as GPT suggests doesn't meaningfully change the meaning. This is a borderline detection.
Matches my detection of the article's failure to include any voices or perspectives supporting gun law changes, creating a one-sided framing.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Australia already has some of the most restrictive gun laws in the world after the government enacted a mandatory buyback program following the 1996 mass shooting in Port Arthur
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the structural framing where the article devotes significant space to emphasizing how strict existing laws already are, implicitly undermining the case for new laws without presenting counterarguments.

Sonnet completely missed the significant omission of any pro-gun-control voices, public safety experts, or victims' perspectives that would provide balance to the article's skeptical framing of new gun laws.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: Australia's prime minister said on Monday that the country's Left-wing government stands "prepared" to pass more restrictive gun laws
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed the 'Left-wing government' label in the body text, which is the author's own ideological characterization repeated beyond just the headline.
